what: The question corpus. Open questions that are LIVE — asked, not answered, and load-bearing on something. A question rests here until it is answered, at which point the answer becomes structure somewhere else (a ticket, a charter, a Law) and the question record freezes with a pointer to where it landed.
why:
/sorted's completeness gate produces two outputs, not one: nodes that pass are cast to `tickets/`, and nodes that come back red are kicked to the question corpus. Without this store the red half has nowhere to go, so it goes into conversation — and conversation is exactly what Law 1 says we must stop re-deriving from. A red at the gate is a FINDING; dropping it wastes the resolver cycles that produced it.

The corpus also makes a red honest. 'Route back to design' with no artifact is indistinguishable from 'we forgot about it,' and a question that dissolves quietly is how a system talks itself into casting the same incomplete node a week later.
owner: each question has exactly one owner who gates writes to it (Law 6). The store owns the collection.

template.owner: the one owner who gates writes to this question
template.question: the question itself, stated so that an answer would be recognizable
template.why_it_is_load_bearing: what is blocked or wrong while this is open — a question that blocks nothing is a musing, not a corpus record
template.reds: for a question kicked back from /sorted: the specific completeness failures, each stated so it can be cleared individually
template.what_would_clear_it: what an answer has to do, not just that one is wanted
template.evidence_so_far: measurements and probes bearing on it (Law 3 — the corpus holds hypotheses labeled as hypotheses)
template.blocks: array — ticket/node ids waiting on this
template.status: OPEN | ANSWERED (with a pointer to where the answer became structure)

notes_on_the_store: A question is never deleted when answered. It freezes with a pointer — the answer's address — because the fact that we once had to ask is itself the provenance of the structure that resulted (Law 5, Law 7).
Named in CLAUDE.md's three-roots table since founding; built 2026-07-22, the first time /sorted actually produced reds that needed somewhere to go. Demanded, not pre-seeded.
provenance: Founded 2026-07-22 during the /sorted run that cast `ticket-and-task` and kicked back two nodes. The store existed as a named destination in the /sorted skill and in CLAUDE.md's roots table but had no directory; the first real kick-back is what demanded it.
